county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Rockville. Created 1821 from unorganized territory (Vigo). Parke County is the 'Covered Bridge Capital of the World' — 31 covered bridges. Bridge construction records document workers. Parke County Historical Society. Sugar Creek and Raccoon Creek valley settlements. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Turkey Run State Park area. Billie Creek Village. Strong Methodist and Christian Church records. Rockville, Montezuma, and Rosedale area records. Wabash and Erie Canal corridor in the eastern portion.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1821. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage applications, licenses, and returns from 1821. Indiana County Clerk issues marriage licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (improving detail over time).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1821. Indiana probate is handled by the Circuit Court (County Clerk). Inventories often list personal property in detail and name heirs.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, order books, and judgments from 1821. Indiana Circuit Court is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, bastardy cases, and naturalization petitions (pre-1906) that identify family relationships. Order books often record road petitions naming landowners.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Parke County Auditor, Rockville / Indiana State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values from 1821. Indiana county auditor records. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Indiana State Archives holds some microfilmed county tax records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax duplicates at Indiana State Archives; others remain at county level

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Parke County. Indiana is a federal land state — most land was sold through federal land offices. Patents record first purchaser from the government. Searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov. Cross-reference with county deed records for subsequent transfers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal records — well-preserved and digitized at BLM-GLO

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Tell City. Created 1814 from Gibson and Warrick counties. Tell City was founded by a Swiss colony (Swiss Colonization Society, 1857) — excellent immigration and colony records at Tell City Historical Society. Perry County Historical Society. Cannelton Cotton Mill (1849) was one of the largest factories in pre-Civil War Indiana — worker records. Ohio River county — steamboat records. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Strong German Catholic and Swiss Reformed church records. Derby and Rome area records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1814. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage applications, licenses, and returns from 1814. Indiana County Clerk issues marriage licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (improving detail over time).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.repository: Perry County Clerk (Circuit/Superior Court), Tell City
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1814. Indiana probate is handled by the Circuit Court (County Clerk). Inventories often list personal property in detail and name heirs.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, order books, and judgments from 1814. Indiana Circuit Court is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, bastardy cases, and naturalization petitions (pre-1906) that identify family relationships. Order books often record road petitions naming landowners.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Perry County Auditor, Tell City / Indiana State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values from 1814. Indiana county auditor records. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Indiana State Archives holds some microfilmed county tax records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax duplicates at Indiana State Archives; others remain at county level

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Perry County. Indiana is a federal land state — most land was sold through federal land offices. Patents record first purchaser from the government. Searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov. Cross-reference with county deed records for subsequent transfers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal records — well-preserved and digitized at BLM-GLO

source_profiles.source_name: German Catholic Parish Records — Perry County
source_profiles.repository: Archdiocese of Indianapolis / Diocese of Evansville / Parish offices
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Baptism, marriage, and burial records from German Catholic parishes. Many parishes kept records in Latin or German. Parish registers often include parents' birthplaces in Germany. St. Meinrad Archabbey (Dubois/Spencer) holds significant church records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Parish records generally well-preserved; some microfilmed by FamilySearch

source_profiles.source_name: Ohio River Commerce Records — Perry County
source_profiles.repository: Indiana State Archives / Local historical societies / Kentucky river communities
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Steamboat records, ferry licenses, river pilot records, and flatboat commerce. Ohio River counties had close economic ties to Kentucky — cross-reference Kentucky county records across the river. Early Indiana settlement came primarily via the Ohio River.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Ferry licenses at county level; steamboat records at NARA and historical societies

source_profiles.repository: Perry County Clerk, Tell City / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Circuit Court (County Clerk). Post-1906 copies at NARA Chicago. Indiana had significant German, Irish, Swiss, and later Eastern European and Mexican immigration.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Indiana naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry; some at Indiana State Archives

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Petersburg. Created 1816 from Gibson and Perry — one of the original counties at statehood. Pike County Historical Society in Petersburg. White River and Patoka River valley settlements. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Coal mining heritage — mine employment records. Winslow and Otwell area records. Strong Baptist and Methodist church records. Gil Hodges (baseball) birthplace area. Check Gibson and Perry for pre-1816 records. Petersburg was an important White River port town.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1816. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1816. Indiana probate is handled by the Circuit Court (County Clerk). Inventories often list personal property in detail and name heirs.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, order books, and judgments from 1816. Indiana Circuit Court is the general trial court. source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values from 1816. Indiana county auditor records. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Indiana State Archives holds some microfilmed county tax records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Pike County. Indiana is a federal land state — most land was sold through federal land offices. source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal records — well-preserved and digitized at BLM-GLO

source_profiles.source_name: Coal Mining Employment Records — Pike County
source_profiles.repository: Indiana State Archives / Indiana Coal Council / Local historical societies
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Indiana coal mine employment records, company town records, and United Mine Workers (UMW) union records. Coal mining attracted Italian, Eastern European, and African American workers. Mine inspection reports at Indiana State Archives list workers. Company store ledgers document families.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Mine records scattered; Indiana Bureau of Mines reports at Indiana State Archives

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Valparaiso. Created 1835 from St. Joseph County. Indiana Dunes National Park (and State Park) area on Lake Michigan. Porter County Historical Society. Valparaiso University (1859) archives. Chesterton and Portage area records. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Kankakee River and Little Calumet River valleys. Swedish immigrant community in Baillytown/Chesterton area — Swedish church records. Porter area steel industry records (Bethlehem Steel). Check St. Joseph for pre-1835 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1835. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage applications, licenses, and returns from 1835. Indiana County Clerk issues marriage licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (improving detail over time).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1835. Indiana probate is handled by the Circuit Court (County Clerk). Inventories often list personal property in detail and name heirs.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Porter County. Indiana is a federal land state — most land was sold through federal land offices. Patents record first purchaser from the government. Searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov. Cross-reference with county deed records for subsequent transfers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal records — well-preserved and digitized at BLM-GLO

source_profiles.source_name: Steel Industry and Immigration Records — Porter County
source_profiles.repository: Calumet Regional Archives, Indiana University Northwest / Indiana State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Gary, East Chicago, Hammond, and Whiting attracted massive immigration for steel and oil refining. U.S. Steel, Inland Steel, and Standard Oil employment records. Serbian, Croatian, Polish, Hungarian, Mexican, and African American (Great Migration) communities. Church records in multiple languages. Calumet Regional Archives holds key collections.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Factory records scattered; Calumet Regional Archives is primary repository. Some records at Indiana University Northwest.

source_profiles.repository: Porter County Clerk, Valparaiso / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Circuit Court (County Clerk). Post-1906 copies at NARA Chicago. Indiana had significant German, Irish, Swiss, and later Eastern European and Mexican immigration.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Indiana naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry; some at Indiana State Archives

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Mount Vernon. Created 1814 from Gibson and Warrick counties. New Harmony was the site of two utopian communities — Harmonie Society (George Rapp, 1814-1825) and Robert Owen's community (1825-1827). New Harmony records are extensive at Indiana State Archives, Working Men's Institute, and New Harmony Athenaeum. Posey County Historical Society. Ohio River and Wabash River confluence area. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Mount Vernon area records. Strong German heritage from Harmonist community.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1814. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Winamac. Created 1835 from Cass and St. Joseph counties. Pulaski County Historical Society. Tippecanoe River valley and Bass Lake area settlements. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Grand Kankakee Marsh area in western portion — drainage records. Francesville and Medaryville area records. Strong Catholic presence — Franciscan monastery in Francesville. Check Cass and St. Joseph for pre-1835 records. Agricultural county with well-preserved but limited early records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1835. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Greencastle. Created 1821 from Vigo and Owen counties. DePauw University (1837) in Greencastle — archives hold local historical materials including the extensive DePauw collection. Putnam County Historical Society and Museum. Big Walnut Creek valley settlements. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Cloverdale and Roachdale area records. Strong Methodist heritage — DePauw was founded by Indiana Methodists. County Clerk holds marriage records from 1822. Bainbridge and Russellville area records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1821. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Indiana provided approximately 197,000 soldiers to the Union Army. Adjutant General records, enlistment papers, and discharge records at Indiana State Archives. Indiana Soldiers and Sailors Monument (Indianapolis). W.H.H. Terrell's 'Report of the Adjutant General of the State of Indiana' (8 vols.) published rosters.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Indiana Adjutant General records well-preserved at Indiana State Archives; indexed online

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Winchester. Created 1818 from Wayne County. Strong Quaker heritage — Randolph County had extensive Friends settlements. Quaker meeting records at Earlham College (Richmond, Wayne County) and Indiana Historical Society. Randolph County Historical Society. White River headwaters area. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Winchester and Farmland area records. Union City straddles the Indiana-Ohio border — cross-reference Darke County, OH. Strong Underground Railroad activity documented. Check Wayne County for pre-1818 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1818. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values from 1818. Indiana county auditor records. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Indiana State Archives holds some microfilmed county tax records.

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Randolph County. Indiana is a federal land state — most land was sold through federal land offices. Patents record first purchaser from the government. Searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov. Cross-reference with county deed records for subsequent transfers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal records — well-preserved and digitized at BLM-GLO

source_profiles.source_name: Society of Friends (Quaker) Meeting Records — Randolph County
source_profiles.repository: Earlham College Friends Collection, Richmond / Indiana Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Monthly meeting records include births, marriages, deaths, removals (transfers), and disownments. Indiana Yearly Meeting and Western Yearly Meeting records. Quakers kept meticulous records. Indiana was a major destination for Quaker migration from the Carolinas and Virginia — check origin meeting records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Exceptionally well-preserved; many microfilmed by FamilySearch and held at Earlham College

source_profiles.source_name: Underground Railroad and African American Records — Randolph County
source_profiles.repository: Indiana State Archives / Indiana Historical Society / Levi Coffin House
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Indiana was a free state but had Black Codes restricting African American settlement. Court records include freedom suits, fugitive slave cases, and Black Code enforcement. Levi Coffin of Fountain City (Wayne County) was the 'President of the Underground Railroad.' Quaker meeting records document abolitionist activity.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Scattered across local historical societies and Indiana State Archives; some published accounts

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Versailles. Created 1816 from Dearborn and Jefferson — one of the original counties at statehood. Very strong German Catholic heritage — Ripley County rivals Dubois County for German settlement. St. Nicholas parish (Sunman) and St. Louis parish (Batesville) records critical. Ripley County Historical Society. Batesville was a significant manufacturing center — Batesville Casket Company and Hill-Rom records. Laughery Creek and Graham Creek valleys. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Milan, Osgood, and Napoleon area records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1816. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Rushville. Created 1821 from unorganized territory (Delaware). Rushville was the home of Wendell Willkie (1940 presidential candidate). Rush County Historical Society. Flatrock River valley settlements. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Strong agricultural county — early plat maps and farm records important. Milroy and Glenwood area records. County Clerk holds marriage records from 1822. Strong Methodist and Christian Church (Disciples of Christ) records. Well-preserved county records from 1822.
